missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Samantha loves theater ... She loves a good lager beer (mem_327f51d0)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 126/362 edges are inactive, many still-true: 'Samantha loves lager beer' SUPERSEDED 'Samantha loves theater' (different objects, no conflict), and 'James has pet Daisy', 'John plays AC Valhalla', 'James visited Josh' are all inactive while parallel facts stay active. Facts present in text are extracted then silently removed from the active graph — same recall failure as never extracting them.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g3-r0 (LoCoMo conv, James/John): 31 records, 2229 claims, 362 edges; every record has 40-119 claims (0 records with zero extraction), so not dead. But claim-type mix is 1799 state / 231 event / 166 preference / 24 interval / 9 quantity — ~81% of claims are untyped sentence-splats ('James | james hey john it | James: Hey John...') that only help lexical search, not typed/deterministic recall. What works: events (adopted/bought/visited), preferences, and relationship edges (Samantha agreed to become James's girlfriend; James has three dogs; James adopted Ned) are present with source attribution. What's broken: numeric values dropped from quantity slots, negation polarity inverted, relative dates never resolved into valid_at (all claims stamp the session time), and the supersession pass expires unrelated still-true edges (126/362 inactive). Repro: DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r14_codex/data, Engine().store, scope namespace above; key records mem_327f51d06f72401f, mem_bd438570fce44f78, mem_4bdb25557bfa4443, mem_d234ead550b84c33.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I was in the final of a big Valorant tournament last Saturday, and I won!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Every claim from a record inherits the session timestamp: the 'won Valorant tournament' event claim has valid_at=2022-11-08 (session date) even though the record's own metadata.dates contains 2022-11-05 (the actual 'last Saturday'). Same for 'Dyed my hair last week - come see!' (valid_at=session date). In a bi-temporal store, value_as_of/as_of ordering and LoCoMo temporal questions ('when did X happen') answer days-wrong while NLI verification still passes, because the quote is entailed but the date field is silently the wrong kind of time.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I made it with coconut milk, vanilla extract, sugar, and a pinch of salt.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 1 of 4 ingredients got a structured claim ([quantity] subj="Nate's dairy-free ice cream recipe" pred='contains coconut milk' val='1'); vanilla extract, sugar, and salt have no typed claim at all. Enumeration questions on the deterministic path (claim_list_enum / structured_recall) undercount, and any sum/count over list members aggregates a partial set -- exactly the measure_sum failure family already flagged as #41/#42.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: Photo-caption facts attributed to a session pseudo-entity instead of the person
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: [shares a photo: a photography of a man with purple hair and glasses taking a selfie]
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Caption claims get subject 'c3_session_7'/'c3_session_10' etc., never the sharer. Nate's hair-dye event claim exists ('Dyed my hair last week') but carries no color, and no claim or edge links Nate to 'purple hair'. Entity-keyed recall for 'what color did Nate dye his hair' finds nothing under Nate; the answer only exists via a read-time join across a pseudo-entity, which the deterministic path won't do. LoCoMo's image-grounded questions hit this class directly.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: Entity attribution on sentence-state claims (pronoun/fragment subjects)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: You mentioned you were allergic to pets earlier, how bad is it?
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: A large share of the 1,552 'state' claims carry junk subjects -- 'You', 'Must', 'Check', 'The', "It's", 'Wanna', 'Bye Nate', 'Something' -- because the subject is the sentence's first token, not the resolved speaker/referent. Any subject-indexed retrieval or graph traversal scoped to 'Nate'/'Joanna' silently drops these rows even though the text was stored verbatim.

raw_extraction.notes: Store: DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r14_codex/data, namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g4-r0 (LoCoMo Nate/Joanna conversation). Counts confirmed: 29 records / 1,936 claims / 227 edges; every record has claims (38-120, roughly proportional to length), so extraction is alive, not dead. Sampled 8 records (shortest mem_88ad66bf585a433f 1319B; longest mem_19f8b779780a4604 6061B; plus mem_ebd51e4fa4224c51, mem_474a5084a0ca4ac6, mem_d6ff9e9e16674651, mem_8959d3a889c54f21, mem_88ddcade32474e7f, mem_d24d8111ea944988). What WORKS: near-total verbatim coverage -- 1,552/1,936 claims are sentence-echo 'state' claims whose val preserves the exact sentence, so lexical/free-read recall can reach almost any fact; key negations/retractions ARE captured (Joanna 'is allergic to' most reptiles/fur + cockroaches as both claims and edges; the used-to-have-a-dog-in-Michigan-then-got-rid-of-her cessation has a dedicated 'had dog in Michigan' claim); headline quantities ARE captured (7 party attendees, 6 would return, turtles owned 3 years, 4 tournaments, rating 9/10, 3 scripts on big screen); edges capture relationships with supersession (Nate owns three turtles supersedes prior edge). What's PATCHY (why not 'healthy'): the typed/deterministic layer the verification and structured_recall paths consume is thin and systematically degraded -- only 9 quantity + 7 interval claims in 1,936, several mis-typed (an 'interval' claim whose val is 'All that hard work and dedication will definitely shine through...'; a 'quantity' whose val is a full sentence); claim valid_at is always session time (relative dates never resolved to event time despite metadata.dates carrying the candidate dates); list members not itemized; photo captions keyed to session pseudo-entities; many subjects are unresolved pronouns/fragments. Also observed: every edge is stored twice (exact duplicate pairs, e.g. 'Joanna is allergic to cockroaches' x2 with different edge_ids), and many low-weight edges are pruned=True. Net: recall that reads raw claim text will do fine; anything entity-keyed, time-keyed, or enumerative works only for the minority of facts that got a real typed claim.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: numeric quantities and ages (typed measure claims)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: my fav memory was when my team won first place at a regionals at age fifteen
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Zero of 1240 claims have unit set and zero have a numeric value; 'age fifteen' is stored as the raw string val='at age fifteen'. Measure-type/sum/comparison queries and deterministic value_as_of cannot operate on prose values -- this is the same numeric-aggregation surface where verify-or-abstain already leaks (issues #41/#42).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Woah, that pic's from when my dance crew took home first in a local comp last year.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Every claim inherits its record's session timestamp (only 19 distinct valid_at values for 1240 claims); 'last year' is anchored to 2023-01-20 instead of 2022, and 'The official opening night is tomorrow' gets valid_at = the day before the event. Bi-temporal as_of recall and event-ordering questions are off by days to a year.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I totally get it, I had a mentor too when I was learning how to dance.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Zero of 163 edges connect Jon and Gina (or Gina to her mentor); the graph is exclusively person->thing. Multi-hop relationship queries (who supports/mentors/is friends with whom) -- a core LoCoMo question class -- have no graph path and fall back to prose matching.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Wanna see my moves next Fri? Can't wait! ... Next Friday works. Let's boogie!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The agreed next-Friday dance session exists only as junk shingles ('[state] Jon | wanna see my moves') with no typed event claim, no participants, no resolved date. 'What did X and Y plan to do' questions cannot be answered via structured_recall and the commitment is invisible to temporal verification.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g5-r0 (Jon/Gina LoCoMo group): 19 records, 1240 claims, 163 edges; sampled 7 records (1525-4516 chars). Extraction is emphatically not dead -- 42-97 claims per record, and the headline biographical facts ARE captured as clean typed claims/edges (Jon lost banker job, Gina lost Door Dash job, competition wins, Rome trip last week, studio grand opening tomorrow, offered services, organizational tools, wholesaler yes). Rated patchy rather than healthy because whole fact classes are structurally absent at the typed layer store-wide, not just in samples: (1) no measure/quantity claim type exists at all (0 units, 0 numeric values in 1240 claims); (2) valid_at is purely session-time (19 distinct values = 1 per record), so relative dates in text are never normalized to event time; (3) the edge graph has zero person-person edges. Caveat: ~60-70% of claim volume is fallback sentence-shingle claims (subject=first token, predicate=slugified sentence prefix, object=whole sentence, e.g. '[state] Jon | jon thanks gina | Jon: Thanks, Gina!') -- these give proof_atom text coverage that may rescue NLI-verified free-read recall, but they inflate the claim count and are useless for structured/deterministic paths. Duplicate edges also observed (each edge appears twice in several records, e.g. 'Jon - offers -> dance classes' x2 in mem_026a1bbc9701467a).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: absolute-date resolution of relative temporal expressions on events
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: By the way, I went to a poetry reading last Fri - it was really powerful!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 9/141 event claims carry an ISO-resolved date; 38 carry only relative phrases ('last Fri', 'a few weeks ago', 'two weekends ago'). Some events do get resolved twins (hiking -> 2023-08-18), but most don't (poetry reading in mem_7555f8bc has no resolved date). LoCoMo temporal questions demand absolute dates, and deterministic/structured recall (value_as_of, temporal filters) cannot answer from an unresolved 'last Fri' without reader-side arithmetic against record valid_at.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: The 2 younger kids love nature.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 3 quantity claims exist in 1512 (all durations like '5 years'). Counts like '2 younger kids' (mem_b6414da1) land only as raw sentence text inside a mistyped preference claim, never as a typed quantity with value/unit. Numeric aggregation is this store's known verified-wrong failure mode (measure_sum over-sums); untyped counts force the reader to re-parse prose and make count questions unverifiable by the deterministic path.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Melanie: Yeah, a buddy of mine adopted last year. It was a long process, but now they're super happy with their new kid.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: This fact (mem_7555f8bc) survives only as a verbatim state claim keyed subject='Melanie', predicate='melanie yeah a buddy' -- no event claim about the friend, no edge (that record has 97 claims, 0 edges). Multi-hop questions like 'whose friend adopted a child?' or 'what happened to Melanie's son?' depend on typed third-party facts; only 1 such edge exists in the graph (Melanie's son -- was in --> accident).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: facts headed by pronouns/fragments (entity resolution to canonical subject)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: It's been a dream to adopt and provide a safe, loving home for kids who need it.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: ~34% of claims (508/1512) are keyed to junk subjects: "It's" (49), 'It' (45), 'Your' (19), 'The' (17), plus session ids. The adoption-dream fact above is stored under subject="It's", not Caroline. Entity-keyed retrieval (structured_recall, fact_history, edges) will never surface these claims for a 'Caroline' query, so the facts are effectively invisible to the deterministic path despite being extracted.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g6-r0 (window artifacts/holdout_rotation_r14_codex): 19 records, 1512 claims, 119 edges; all 19 records have 41-131 claims each, so extraction is HEALTHY by volume -- the failure modes are typing/normalization, not absence. Claim-type mix is heavily skewed: state 1219, event 141, preference 137, interval 12, quantity 3. Sentence-level shredding gives near-total textual recall (23/24 negation sentences and all 11 numeric sentences appear inside some claim's value), so free-read retrieval works; the four classes above are where the TYPED layer (claims/edges the deterministic verify path uses) misses. Edges are sparse but real (owns->Oliver/Luna pets, is from->Sweden, married->5 years ago, plays->clarinet); 4 of 19 records have 0 edges including the 97-claim adoption conversation. Sampled records: mem_c501e21c (shortest, 1894c/41cl), mem_7330ccfb (longest, 6464c/131cl/14e), mem_7555f8bc (4406c/97cl/0e), mem_31402d8c, mem_b6414da1, mem_ac445438, mem_b37c7d96.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I've been working on it for two years now, so there have been ups and downs, but I'm doing my best. (mem_46503556)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 1 quantity claim exists in all 1600 claims, and the 11 interval claims are mostly misparses ('[interval] Evan | were gorgeous | gorgeous'). Duration/count questions ('how long has Evan been prioritizing his health?') have no typed substrate, and numeric aggregation is exactly where verify-or-abstain already leaks (the 2026-07-09 headline) -- untyped quantities force the fuzzy text path with no deterministic check.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Sam: Hey Evan, haven't found any exercises I like. (mem_bab7351f) -- extracted as [preference] Sam | like | 'haven't found any exercises I like'
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The negated statement is stored as a positive 'like' preference claim; structured_recall/preference_profile would assert the opposite of the source. Retraction/negation handling is a first-class product claim ('a negated assertion answers No'), so polarity inversion at extraction silently defeats it and can pass NLI since the source text is embedded verbatim in the object.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: As for low-impact exercises, swimming, yoga, and walking are good options. (mem_81f7bdf6) -- extracted as [state] As | are good | 'good options'
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Subject parsed as the word 'As' and the three list items vanish from subject/predicate/object, so enumeration questions ('which low-impact exercises did Evan suggest?') cannot be answered from claims. The cost-blitz claim_list_enum carve-out makes itemized lists a load-bearing cheap-path class; coverage is inconsistent (same class captured fine in mem_9360a3c1 'skiing, snowboarding, and ice skating').

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Last weekend, I took my family on a road trip to Jasper. (mem_487ed7ee) -- event claim '[event] Evan | took | family on a road trip to Jasper' has anchors=[] and no event_date filter
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Records carry dates metadata (e.g. mem_e38d7661 has 3 parsed dates) but almost no event claim inherits them -- sampled event claims show anchors=[] and only rare filters like event_date='2023-05-18'. LoCoMo 'when did X happen' questions and the bi-temporal as_of path cannot resolve against typed claims, forcing free-read on the class the benchmark tests hardest.

raw_extraction.notes: Store confirmed at 25 records / 1600 claims / 150 edges for namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g7-r0 (DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r14_codex/data). Every record produced claims (min 40, max 103) so extraction is alive, and headline durable facts (marriage, honeymoon, Weight Watchers, Jasper trip, ginger-snap limit) do get claims -- hence patchy, not dead. But volume is inflated by noise: 543/1600 claims (34%) have non-person subjects ('It's' x54, 'Can't', 'Keep', 'c8_session_5' photo-caption claims), and many person-subject claims are verbatim sentence echoes with garbled predicates ('sam morning evan'), i.e. the extractor is a sentence-splitter more than a fact-typer. Edges are also patchy: 6/25 records have 0 edges (mem_dfa91540, mem_487ed7ee, mem_bab7351f, mem_46503556, mem_81f7bdf6, mem_39bbc9b8) even when clear relations exist in text ('I met this awesome Canadian woman' in mem_46503556; Evan's partner in mem_bab7351f), and every emitted edge is duplicated exactly twice (150 edges is ~75 unique). Claim-type distribution: state 1351, event 131, preference 106, interval 11, quantity 1 -- the typed classes that enable deterministic recall (quantity/interval/event-with-date) are near-empty. Sampled records: mem_298ff7ca, mem_dfa91540, mem_487ed7ee, mem_bab7351f, mem_46503556, mem_81f7bdf6, mem_e38d7661, mem_9360a3c1; raw text and claim dumps in scratchpad (records_raw.txt, claims_dump.txt). Notable contrast: mem_e38d7661 and mem_9360a3c1 (longest records) each have a handful of high-quality typed claims (e.g. '[quantity] Evan | limiting ginger snaps per day | two ginger snaps a day') sitting on top of the same junk layer, suggesting a second, better extractor runs only sometimes -- the coverage gap is whichever pass produced those clean claims not firing on the other records.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: dated events with relative time expressions (unresolved event dates)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I booked a flight ticket to Boston last week!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Namespace-wide, 35 of 45 unique relative-time proof atoms ('last week', 'yesterday', 'next month', 'last Friday') have NO claim with a resolved event_date filter (only 11 of 67 such claims are dated). The claim for this quote stores value='last week' literally and falls back to the session valid_at. LoCoMo temporal questions ('when did X happen', ordering across sessions) and value_as_of/fact_history time travel cannot answer from these claims -- the exact class of question this benchmark grades hardest.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I've never been to Boston before, but I hear the parks are amazing.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0 of 2107 claims carry a negation type; the 28 negation-bearing atoms survive only as junk echo claims ('calvin nah haven t') or, worse, this quote became a POSITIVE preference claim 'Calvin | are amazing | Boston before...'. False-premise defense and conflict detection go blind: another record in this same namespace has Calvin saying 'I was there [Boston] a few years back' -- the contradiction is undetectable without a structured negative.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Ever since I was ten, I've been fascinated with how machines work
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Exactly 1 of 2107 claims has a numeric value or unit. This quote was extracted as garbage: 'state | Dave | was ten | ten, ...'. Given the known live failure class (verify-or-abstain does not protect numeric aggregation, issues #41/#42), there is nothing structured to sum, count, or compare -- every quantity question must fall back to raw re-reading.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: disposal / state-change events that should supersede prior ownership
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I restored a car last year, but I sold it to a collector.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 'restored' got a structured event claim (and a 'Dave | restored | red car' edge from another record) but 'sold it to a collector' exists only as a sentence echo -- no sold event, no invalid_at on the ownership state. 'Does Dave still have the car' answers from the stale positive claim; the bi-temporal supersession machinery never fires because the terminating event was never structured.

raw_extraction.notes: Sampled 8/30 records (longest mem_3e95be6eada04314 6602ch/129 claims; shortest mem_ad3c6ede2cfa45e4 1510ch/37 claims; plus 1b53c578, 4a9c4fce, 65ce8f40, fcebb4f4, e110931f, 8965f369). Raw textual coverage is near-total: every non-question sentence yields at least one claim (uncovered sentences per record = 0-10, all conversational questions), so recall-by-text and NLI verification have material to work with -- extraction is NOT dead. But roughly two-thirds of the 2107 claims are low-signal speaker-echo copies (subjects like \"It's\", \"Keep\", \"Take\", \"c9_session_28\"; predicates like \"dave thanks calvin\"), inflating the count while the structured fields deterministic paths query are absent. Claim-type mix: 1779 state / 170 event / 139 preference / 19 interval, 0 negation. Edge coverage is bimodal: 190 edges total but 6/30 records have ZERO edges (mem_1b53c578f8bf4b6d, mem_65ce8f40ae1b43e6, mem_73e4f07f1cb2468a, mem_4a9c4fce222c44ed, mem_fcebb4f437cd450b, mem_ad3c6ede2cfa45e4) despite clear relational facts in their text (e.g. booked-flight-to-Boston, met-artists-in-Boston); some of those relations (Frank Ocean tour) are recovered from OTHER records, so entity-level graph coverage partially self-heals across sessions. Verdict 'patchy': facts are present as text but four whole classes (resolved dates, negation polarity, numerics, disposal/supersession events) are missing in structured form. Repro scripts: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-raunakgengiti-Eidetic-Plus/eb09f70d-9fc9-40be-884b-dc6507a26ca8/scratchpad/audit1.py, audit3.py, audit4.py (run with DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r14_codex/data).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: dated events (relative-date resolution into claim event time)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Last year, I helped renovate a rundown community center back home.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0 of 2161 claims have valid_at differing from the record's session timestamp (>1 day), even though record metadata already carries resolved dates (mem_7b10ed034d064398 metadata dates includes '2022-01-01T00:00:00' for this exact sentence). Every 'last week'/'last month'/'last year' event is stamped at conversation time, so as_of/value_as_of time travel and any 'when did X happen' temporal question answers with the session date, not the event date.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I lost my job at the mechanical engineering company. They tanked and it's been really rough.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0 active claims in the whole namespace carry invalid_at; the earlier still-active claim 'I'm really enjoying my new job.' (valid_at 2023-07-23) coexists with the job-loss event (2023-08-06) with no invalidation link. Latest-assertion-wins and verify-or-abstain contradiction handling have nothing to key on at the claim layer, so a stale employment/state claim remains citable with verified=True. (Edges do get expired -- 34/174 -- but claims never do.)

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: John: I set up a 5K charity run in our neighborhood.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 1 'quantity' claim and 5 unit-bearing claims exist out of 2161 (Kyle age 1yr, workout 3x/week, Max 10 years, shelter 1x/month, 2 hours). Numerics like '5K charity run' and 'a little girl around 8' survive only as untyped sentence-echo state claims, so the deterministic structured_recall / measure-sum path (the repo's known numeric-aggregation weak spot, issues #41/#42) has no typed values to aggregate or verify.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g9-r0 (window artifacts/holdout_rotation_r14_codex): 32 records, 2161 active claims, 174 edges confirmed. Sampled 6 records across the length range (mem_b1b8787556d24a51 1954ch/40 claims, mem_ea078fea7ed94199 2131ch/44, mem_7b10ed034d064398 2383ch/56, mem_6de216ad90f74c82, mem_1824a2c582d14dcd 4930ch/98, mem_cee9b5b5486f41dc 5802ch/118). Coverage is effectively per-sentence: every substantive fact appears in at least one claim, and key typed events are present (John lost job -> mechanical engineering company; John got promoted -> assistant manager; Maria joined gym val=2023-06-09; Maria tried surfing; John works out 3x/week with unit). Edges are healthy relational facts (Max died, Maria adopted Shadow, 5K run benefited veterans); 27/32 records have >=1 edge; 12 pruned + 34 expired edges show edge-layer supersession works. Claim-type mix: state 1820 (84%), event 194, preference 127, interval 19, quantity 1. Quality caveat (not a coverage gap, so not listed as a missed class): the dominant state claims are verbatim sentence echoes with token-soup predicates ('john thanks maria', 'maria wow john it') and junk subjects ('Long', \"That's\", 'Keep', \"Here's\", 'c2_session_13' for photo captions), which pollutes subject-indexed claim lookup and fragments entity resolution while still guaranteeing recall coverage. Zero-extraction is not applicable; note that MemoryRecord.content is empty -- the text lives in the .text attribute (raw bytes in CAS), which can make naive audits report false zero extraction.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: dated events with relative temporal expressions (event-time anchoring)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Guess what - I got a puppy two weeks ago!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0/2320 claims have a valid_at that differs from the record's conversation timestamp — 'two weeks ago', 'last week' (flood), 'last month' (boot camps, sunset), 'yesterday' are all stamped at mention time, not event time. Temporal QA ('when did Maria get the puppy') and value_as_of/fact_history time travel answer with the wrong date, and any two events mentioned in one session are temporally indistinguishable, so cross-session ordering questions can be verified-wrong through NLI (the citation text still entails the answer).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Thanks, Maria! Three times a week; it keeps us on track.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 4/2320 claims are typed 'quantity' and only 2 carry a unit; this fact landed as [interval] 'John and his family | work out together | three times a week | unit=None' and 'important part of our family for 10 years' landed as a quantity claim with garbage subject/predicate ('John | was such'). The deterministic numeric path (structured_recall / measure-type sum) has no parsed value+unit to aggregate or compare — exactly the surface behind the 2026-07-09 'verify-or-abstain does not protect numeric aggregation' headline (#41/#42).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: speaker attribution — first-person facts keyed to non-entity subjects
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Just started doing aerial yoga, it's great.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 409/2320 claims (18%) have junk subjects ('Just', "It's", 'Your', "Let's", 'Keep'...). Maria's signature hobby here is keyed to subject 'Just', so subject-keyed retrieval (fact_history('Maria'), value_as_of, subject-filtered structured_recall) misses it entirely even though the sentence text is stored; only free-text/NLI recall can find these facts.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relationship edges for new entities (graph coverage gap: 14/32 records have zero edges)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Her name is Shadow!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Maria's new puppy Shadow has claims but no ownership edge; the graph's dog facts are the deceased dog Max ('Max was a dog') and 'John plans to adopt rescue dog', so multi-hop/bridge-join queries about Maria's current pet resolve to stale entities. 44% of records (including the longest, 5,802 chars / 117 claims / 0 edges — no John-[attends]->boot camp) contribute nothing to the graph; edges skew John (88 src) vs Maria (44 src).

raw_extraction.notes: Store access confirmed via DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data, namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g0-r0: 32 records, 2320 active claims, 188 non-inferred edges (matches target). Per-record coverage is dense and uniform: 42-117 claims per record, roughly one claim per sentence, zero records with no claims — so verbatim/NLI recall is well fed and the verdict is 'patchy' rather than 'dead'. Negations are NOT a missed class: they are captured and even pronoun-resolved (e.g. 'No, I didn't' -> state claim 'Maria | did not have pets growing up'). Claim-type distribution: state 1896, event 262, preference 134, interval 24, quantity 4; units on 2/2320. The four classes above are the consequential gaps: they don't lose the text, they lose the TYPED form (event-time valid_at, value+unit, entity subject, graph edge) that the deterministic/verification paths depend on. Sampled records: mem_acc0, mem_95ab (shortest), mem_5f69, mem_79ba, mem_1451 (longest), plus targeted probes (puppy mem_995e, negation mem_31f1, dog-loss mem_9ec3) across all 32. Probe scripts at /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-raunakgengiti-Eidetic-Plus/eb09f70d-9fc9-40be-884b-dc6507a26ca8/scratchpad/{audit.py,dump_ns.py}.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I noticed they weren't acting normally, so I made an appointment with an animal behaviorist last Wed. (mem_bc6a7ae8)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0 of 2304 claims have a valid_at different from the source record's session timestamp and 0 claims carry anchors, yet 19/28 records contain relative-date phrases (last Wed, Last Friday, next month, yesterday). Event claims exist but event time == mention time, so temporal questions ('when did Audrey see the behaviorist?') and the bi-temporal value_as_of/as_of path cannot resolve or verify 'when' answers -- the whole date burden falls to the free-read reader.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Andrew: ...Saturday works or Monday? ... Audrey: Saturday works for me! (mem_bc6a7ae8); Let's do it next month when the weather is more pleasant. (mem_c05d9485)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The agreed hike date survives only as utterance-echo state claims ('Audrey: Saturday works for me!'), never as an event claim with a future valid_at. Cross-session event ordering and 'when are they planning to X' recall have no structured target, and a later session's actual hike cannot supersede/confirm the plan.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: itemized lists not enumerated into per-item claims
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: We give them lots of activities to keep them busy - puzzles, training, hide-and-seek - they love it all! (mem_e81c8f4b)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Claim types in this namespace are only state/preference/event/interval/quantity -- no list/enum family despite the itemized-list P1b claim family existing elsewhere in the repo. Lists are stored as one preference blob, so deterministic structured_recall cannot enumerate items ('what activities...'), forcing the expensive free-read path and making completeness unverifiable.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: per-entity attributes stated in grouped sentences (distributive extraction)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: They're all mutts, but Pepper and Panda are Lab mixes, and Precious and Pixie are Chihuahua mixes. (mem_bc6a7ae8)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Breed is captured only at group level (subject='Audrey's dogs', object='Pepper and Panda'); there is no subject=Pepper/Panda breed claim or edge anywhere in the store, and mem_bc6a7ae8 (the longest record, 5466 chars) produced 0 edges. Entity-keyed lookups ('what breed is Pepper?') miss, and the conflicting group edge from mem_19b3c5a0 ('Audrey's dogs --include--> Jack Russell mixes') can never be reconciled per dog.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g1-r0 (window artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex, DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data): 28 records, 2304 claims, 205 base edges. Every record extracted (49-133 claims each; min at mem_ded63ac8=49, max at mem_c05d9485=133), so not dead/patchy at the record level. Sampled 6 records across the size range (mem_bc6a7ae8 5466, mem_c05d9485 4842, mem_e81c8f4b 3105, mem_ded63ac8 1916, plus edge-bearing mem_19b3c5a0/mem_465d2d98). Strengths: all 7 pet names (Pepper/Panda/Precious/Pixie/Toby/Buddy/Max) appear as edge entities; quantities/frequencies well covered (4 dogs, 3-hour drive, once a week, twice a week, 3 years); negations represented ('has never done fishing' edge, 'Haven't been to the beach' state). Caveats beyond the 4 classes: (1) roughly half the 1870 'state' claims are verbatim utterance echoes with junk subjects ('Here's', 'It's', 'Can't', 'Andrew: Yup!') -- token cost and retrieval noise rather than coverage; (2) 13/28 records produced 0 edges, including the two longest, so edge coverage is record-lumpy even though entity coverage recovers via other records; (3) one verified-wrong edge spotted in passing: 'Andrew --has girlfriend--> Toby' (Toby is his dog, mem_ea987a14) and contradictory 'Audrey --has dogs--> two dogs' (mem_f4d79a63) vs quantity=4 claims -- accuracy, not coverage, but relevant to the verify-or-abstain headline. Claim filters (1671 populated) hold LoCoMo QA metadata (question/answer_type), not scope filters.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Jolene: I'm keen on two projects in particular. One is focused on developing renewable energy, like solar... The other is finding ways to supply clean water to those with limited access.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Item 1 is extracted as subj='One' pred='is focused' (person lost); item 2 has NO claim bound to Jolene at all; the anchor claim truncates to obj='particular'. A recall like 'which two projects is Jolene interested in?' cannot be answered from typed claims -- exactly the itemized-list P1b family the cost blitz targeted. (mem_8235e488975c4a62)

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relative dates never resolved to absolute event time
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Deborah: Hey Jolene, sorry to tell you this but my dad passed away two days ago.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 13 claims store the raw phrase ('two days ago', 'Two weeks ago', 'Three years ago', 'last year') as value with valid_at pinned to the SESSION timestamp (death claim valid_at=2023-01-27, true event 2023-01-25; 'Three years ago...Rio de Janeiro' gets valid_at=2023-08-30, true event ~2020). Only 5 claims namespace-wide got ISO-resolved. Temporal 'when did X happen' questions and value_as_of/as_of time travel silently answer with the wrong era.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: [shares a photo: a photo of two women in a dance studio doing a dance pose]
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Of ~130 photo-share claims, only 18 have a person subject (Deborah 10, Jolene 8, plus 1 misattributed to the pet Seraphim); the rest carry subj='c7_session_N'. LoCoMo asks 'what photo did X share?' -- those claims cannot bind the image content to the speaker, so the answer path falls back to free-read or abstains.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: numeric quantities left as prose, not typed quantity claims
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Jolene: I'm using the Pomodoro Technique - 25 minutes work, 5-minute break - to avoid burnout
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 9 quantity claims exist among 1876 (pet ages and '3 years of yoga' made it; 25/5 minutes, 'been together for three years' interval-only, etc. survive solely inside value strings). The deterministic/structured path (structured_recall, measure_sum) is blind to un-typed numbers -- the exact numeric-aggregation surface the 2026-07-09 headline flagged as verify-or-abstain's weak spot.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g2-r0 (LoCoMo dialog transcripts, Jolene/Deborah). Every one of the 30 records extracted (41-144 claims each; no dead records). Sampled 6 records in depth (mem_e358603a42724d42 6385ch, mem_f43dbbfca51e42e7 refs, mem_9fdb0bb71bc64a06 1573ch, mem_1d4dda7a2f64412b, mem_8235e488975c4a62, mem_e0020ed1d6bf4a04) and probed ~12 high-value facts across the rest. The semantic tier catches almost everything substantive: retraction/cancellation ('Storm forced us to cancel our yoga getaway' -> event claim), negation ('We're not married yet but we've been together for three years' -> interval + quantity 'three years' + verbatim state), deaths + dated events ('my parents' wedding in 1993' -> occurred_in 1993), possessions/gifts, 'Battlefield 1 last week', 'won three large pizzas' (typed quantity), Nintendo at age 10, Pomodoro/Eisenhower adoption, Rio trips for both speakers. Structural caveats: (1) ~980/1876 (52%) of claims are shingle-fallback junk (subj='Good'/'It'/'That's', predicate=word-shingle, full-utterance echoes) -- pure noise/token cost, inflates the 1876 count; real semantic coverage is ~900 claims. (2) One misattribution found: subj='Jolene' pred='nickname' obj='Deb' (Deb is Deborah's nickname) -- correctness, not coverage. (3) Duplicated semantic claims (e.g. Avalanche read-event twice). (4) Edges are sparse and lumpy: 22/30 records have 0 edges; edge extraction only fired on 8 records (relations like has_pet, married_in, died, practices), so graph-path recall depends on which session happened to get edges. Claim-type mix: state 1521, event 192, preference 143, interval 11, quantity 9.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: event-time resolution of relative dates (dated events anchored to session time, never backdated)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I was in the final of a big Valorant tournament last Saturday, and I won! (mem_0941be3eb0794c77)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The interval claim stores the literal string 'last Saturday' but valid_at=1667880600 (the session date, 2022-11-08). 0 of 29 records have even one claim whose valid_at differs from the record's session timestamp (29 distinct valid_at values for 2067 claims = one per record). Deterministic time-travel (value_as_of / as_of recall) and LoCoMo 'when did X happen' questions will return the session date, not the event date — a verified-wrong answer class, same failure shape as the known numeric-aggregation leak.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: It turns out they live close by. We decided to do doggy playdates (mem_c37e0d83c1844eba)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The only claim carrying this fact is [state] subj='It' pred='it turns out they' — the neighbor couple is never resolved into an entity, so no structured claim or edge links Nate to the couple or their location. ~257/2067 claims (12%) have pronoun/fragment/photo-echo subjects ('It's', 'Your', 'Keep', 'He', c3_session_N); substantive facts that appear ONLY in these echoes are unreachable via the typed-claim (structured_recall) path and pollute subject-indexed retrieval.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: interpersonal transfer directionality (gifts/giving mis-attributed as receiving)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Looky here, I got this new pup for you! ... It's a stuffed animal to remind you of the good vibes. (mem_c37e0d83c1844eba)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Extracted as [event] Nate | receive | new pup — direction inverted and recipient Joanna dropped. The 'gift from' predicate family is also garbage where it fires (e.g. 'Nate | gift from | make coconut milk icecream and | it a try'). Who-gave-what-to-whom questions get confidently wrong structured answers rather than abstentions; Joanna's ownership only recovers incidentally via a later record ('Joanna has a stuffed animal dog | Tilly').

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: photo/image content attribution to the sharer
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: [shares a photo: a photo of a stuffed animal laying on a bed] (mem_c37e0d83c1844eba)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Photo captions are stored as echo claims under synthetic subjects (c3_session_7, c3_session_13, c3_session_27) rather than attributed to the person who shared them; only occasionally does a derived person-claim appear (e.g. 'Nate has purple hair'). LoCoMo image-grounded questions ('what photo did Nate share?') can't be answered from the subject-indexed claim path — only via free-text search over the echo.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g3-r0 (LoCoMo Nate/Joanna dialogues): 29 records (1.3k-6.1k chars), 2067 claims, 157 edges; every record has claims (43-133, roughly proportional to length), zero orphan claims. Sampled 6 records across the size range plus keyword probes over the full store. Coverage of core fact classes is genuinely good: quantities (10 typed quantity claims: gaming party 7 attendees/6 repeats, 4th tournament won, 3rd movie on screen, turtles owned 3 years), negations/constraints (83 negation-flavored claims incl. 'Joanna cannot have dairy', 'is allergic to most reptiles and animals with fur' with 6 allergic_to edges, 'does not have a vacation lined up'), preferences (214 claims: 'big fan of Nintendo games', favorite-genre edges), relationships ('Joanna's brother wrote a handwritten letter', 'Nate has a dog named Max', Max is adopted), locations ('near Fort Wayne'), itemized lists (dessert-ingredient edges: dairy-free chocolate cake -> almond flour/coconut oil/chocolate). Edges are sparse and concentrated (only 12/29 records produce any; 17 records have 0 edges) but claims compensate. Health = healthy, with two systemic caveats: (1) bi-temporal event-time is never used — all claims inherit session ingest time, relative dates survive only as literal strings inside interval claims, so as_of/time-travel answers are session-dated; (2) ~12% of claims are sentence-echo/pronoun-subject noise, a precision problem that also hides a few facts (e.g. 'they live close by') that exist ONLY in echo form. Reproduce: DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data, Engine().store.{all_records,active_claims_at,all_edges} with Scope(namespace='eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g3-r0'); key exhibit records: mem_98623643f1974ad3 (shortest, echo noise), mem_0941be3eb0794c77 (longest, best extraction), mem_c37e0d83c1844eba (gift inversion + unresolved couple).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Last week I scored 40 points, my highest ever, and it feels like all my hard work's paying off
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: All 2423 claims inherit the session timestamp: 0/29 records have >1 distinct valid_at, and only 6 claims carry a resolved ISO date (in value, under vague predicates 'go'/'trip', not valid_at). 34 relative-date mentions ('last week', 'next month') are never resolved to per-event times, so temporal recall ('when did John score 40 points?') and as_of/value_as_of time travel answer with the session date, systematically off by the stated offset — and NLI verification cannot catch a date the source only entails relatively (same failure family as the #41/#42 numeric-aggregation leak).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Next month, I'm off to Ireland for a semester.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The only typed claim is [state] Tim 'is going to Ireland' val='True' (mem_f17f0f7d93f743be) — both 'next month' (when) and 'for a semester' (how long) are dropped. Duration/when questions on the deterministic structured_recall path get a bare boolean and must abstain or fall back to raw text; the interval claim type (23 total) never captured it.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: [shares a photo: a photo of a collection of harry potter books on a desk]
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Photo captions are extracted with the SESSION as subject (subj='c4_session_27') instead of the sharer (Tim, mem_fb40ce42305f4095); attribution to a person is inconsistent record-to-record. LoCoMo image-grounded questions ('what did Tim show John?') cannot be answered by subject-keyed claim lookup, and dozens of session-subject caption claims pollute retrieval.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I bet you were thrilled when everything finally worked out.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Claim extracted as [state] subj='Tim' 'were thrilled' (mem_ccdef4ff6b9c4db8) — the fact is about John but is attributed to the speaker Tim; many claims land on grammatical tokens as subjects ('You'll', "It's", 'Must', 'Keep'). Wrong-subject claims are worse than missing ones: they can pass verification while answering about the wrong person.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g4-r0 (Tim/John LoCoMo conv): 29 records, 2423 claims, 216 edges (target said 219; all_edges include_inferred=False returns 216). Extraction is alive on every record (47-141 claims each, scaling with length); sampled records mem_3a1e29736931474d, mem_fb40ce42305f4095, mem_ccdef4ff6b9c4db8, mem_0f6d4a45601f41fd in full plus keyword probes on mem_f17f0f7d93f743be, mem_7d4fd0a84d454cc8, mem_7a95b4cf066745da. Substantive coverage is good: endorsement deal, 40-point game, HP conference in the UK, trophy win, languages, favorites (HP/Star Wars/LOTR/Aragorn), Barcelona recommendation (edge Barcelona-known_for->'culture, architecture, food, beaches'), third-party Anthony captured in claims. Durations partially covered via 10 quantity claims ('four years teammates', 'about four months violin'). No retractions/negations exist in this window's text, so that class is untestable here. Caveats: (1) precision is poor — roughly half the claims are junk (tokenized predicates like 'tim hey john', greeting/ack/photo-caption claims, subjects like \"It's\"/'Keep'), edges are frequently duplicated 2x; (2) the one systemic COVERAGE gap is claim-level event-time resolution — record.metadata['dates'] contains resolved dates (e.g. ['2024-01-05T13:41:00','2024-01-01T00:00:00']) but claims never get them as valid_at, so the bi-temporal machinery has nothing to bite on within a session. That is the highest-leverage fix for LoCoMo temporal-reasoning questions.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Caroline: I started playing acoustic guitar about five years ago; it's been a great way to express myself and escape into my emotions.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 3 of 1688 claims are claim_type='quantity'. Durations/counts live as prose inside state/interval values ('five years ago', 'ten years', 'once or twice a year') with empty or ad-hoc unit fields. The deterministic numeric path (measure/sum, duration comparison, value_as_of) cannot aggregate prose values -- this is exactly the numeric-aggregation class that the 2026-07-09 verify-or-abstain headline says leaks (5/13 verified-WRONG).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: resolved absolute dates for events (all 9 ISO-resolved claims are Caroline 'go/visit/join'; zero for Melanie or non-motion events)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Melanie: Yeah, I made it in pottery class yesterday.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 49 event/interval claims still carry raw relative expressions ('yesterday', 'last Fri', 'last month', 'next month') as their value; only 9 claims namespace-wide got resolved ISO dates (e.g. go->hiking 2023-08-18), and none of Melanie's events (pottery class, homeless-shelter volunteering, pottery workshop, injury) were resolved. Temporal questions ('when did Melanie make the plate?') require a reader-side join against record valid_at; the deterministic time-travel path misses them entirely.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relationship graph edges -- 10 of 19 records emit zero edges
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Melanie: Marrying my partner and promising to be together forever was the best part.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The marriage fact exists as a claim ('Melanie married her partner') but there is NO spouse/partner edge anywhere in the namespace, and mem_9d377c4cc2d64cbc (6198 chars, 153 claims) produced 0 edges. Multi-hop/bridge-join graph recall over people relations cannot traverse what was never edged; edge coverage is per-record all-or-nothing (records that do have edges also duplicate every edge ~2x).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: structured negation polarity (negations captured only as raw utterances or degenerate predicates)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Caroline: Nah, I haven't.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: This reply (Caroline has not tried pottery) got only a raw-utterance state claim (pred='caroline nah i haven'). Where negations are structured at all they use string predicates like 'havent'/'dont' -- ClaimRecord has no polarity field. The retraction/false-premise answer path ('No -- <premise>') cannot key off polarity, so catching negated facts falls back to NLI, the known weak spot.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g5-r0 in artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data: 19 records / 1688 claims / 169 edges confirmed. Every record has claims (min 49, max 153; roughly proportional to text length 1894-6464 chars), so extraction ran end-to-end on all records. Sampled 8 records in depth: mem_85ad4bb6748d4215 (shortest), mem_89acbb01a8404c36 (longest, full dump), mem_9d377c4cc2d64cbc (largest zero-edge record, full dump), plus targeted checks on mem_dc40dbba0a994c42, mem_af864e42c80f4ff5, mem_a739b6661f0b4fc0, mem_9dee2747ecf24d1d, mem_4ef507f8ab60431f. Substantive fact spot-checks nearly all HIT at the claim layer: dated events (LGBTQ support group, conference, council meeting, pride parade, hiking, beach), durations (transitioning 3 yrs, guitar 5 yrs, married 5 yrs, art since 17, bowl 10 yrs), counts (2 younger kids), frequencies (beach once/twice a year), negations (haven't done pottery, missed pride parade, took a break from pottery after injury), preferences (drawing flowers, painting landscapes), relationships (marriage, kids, friends-4-years). Claim-type mix: state 1320 / event 197 / preference 151 / interval 17 / quantity 3. Verdict is healthy at the claim layer -- no fact class is wholly absent -- but with two systemic normalization gaps (quantity typing near-zero; relative-date resolution limited to one speaker's motion verbs) and a patchy edge layer (10/19 records zero edges, duplicates where present). Caveats: extraction is noisy-exhaustive -- a large fraction of the 1688 claims are junk raw-utterance states (e.g. subj='Melanie' pred='melanie hey caroline', subj=\"You've\" pred='you ve got guts'), so raw claim counts overstate signal; and photo-share captions are extracted as claims attributed to session subjects (c0_session_N), occasionally misattributing artifacts (Caroline 'made three stained glass windows' derived from a photo caption). Inspection scripts left in scratchpad: rec_dump.py, scan.py, check.py, neg.py, neg2.py, rel.py, dates.py.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: temporally-resolved dated events (relative-date resolution to claim anchors)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: By the way, yesterday I went bowling and got 2 strikes. I love bowling!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The claim exists ([event] James | went bowling | yesterday) but anchors=[] on ALL 2161 claims in the namespace, and edges carry only session-time valid_at (e.g. every edge from mem_0bf02576 has identical valid_at=1647557220.0), never the resolved event date. Record metadata has resolved ISO 'dates' arrays but nothing propagates to claims. Bi-temporal recall (value_as_of, as_of, 'when did James go bowling') cannot answer deterministically -- 84 claims contain unresolved 'ago/yesterday/last/next' strings, so every LoCoMo-style temporal question falls back to the reader or abstains.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relationship edges for 39% of records (12 of 31 have zero edges)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Look, I was playing a game and my faithful furry friend Daisy came and lay down next to me. This is so cute!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: mem_b13123d5 is the LONGEST record (4983 bytes, 99 claims) yet produced 0 edges -- no James-owns->Daisy, no John-plays->AC Valhalla, no book-recommendation edge, despite mem_0bf02576 producing 26 edges for comparable content. Edge extraction is all-or-nothing per record (silent per-ingest failure, not content-driven), so graph-path recall (fact_history, value_as_of, multi-hop) is blind to 12 whole sessions. Also 13 of 231 edge rows are exact duplicates (same src/relation/dst/source_memory_id).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Last Tuesday I met three cool new friends in my programming course
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only 12 of 2161 claims (0.6%) are claim_type=quantity, and one of those ([quantity] CS:GO tournament | raised money for | a dog shelter, mem_a36a4735) contains no number at all. Counts like 'three new friends' or '2 strikes' survive only as prose inside event/state text. This directly feeds the 2026-07-09 headline (verify-or-abstain ships verified-WRONG numeric aggregations): the deterministic measure/sum path has almost no typed numeric claims to operate on, so counting questions route through error-prone generation.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: James: Yeah, VR gaming is awesome! Let`s do it next Saturday!
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The only extraction is a garbage echo claim -- [state] Let | let s do it | Let`s do it next Saturday! -- with subject 'Let', so it is unretrievable by entity (John/James) and the date is unresolved. Same for the joint travel plan ('I'll start looking for a country where we can go'), which gets a claim on James only. Prospective questions ('what did John and James plan to do together, and when?') have no first-class claim binding both entities to the activity and date.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g6-r0 in artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data: 31 records, 2161 claims, 231 base edges (store returned 231 vs manifest 232; 13 duplicate edge rows). Extraction is alive on every record (38-136 claims each; metadata claims_extracted matches actual counts, one exception mem_0bf02576: meta 121 vs 136 stored). Rated PATCHY not healthy because three whole classes are systematically absent (claim-level temporal anchors: 0/2161; edges: 0 for 12/31 records; typed quantities: 12/2161), even though most substantive facts do get at least one text-level claim. Secondary quality signal (noise, not coverage): 1740/2161 claims are 'state' and a large fraction are transcript-echo junk with unretrievable subjects ('It's', 'Bet', 'Hiccups', 'c6_session_8', predicate 'shares a photo a'), plus at least one misattribution ([state] John | heard of "The Name of the Wind" -- in the text John is the recommender and James had never heard of it). Sampled records: mem_b13123d5 (4983B/99c/0e), mem_0bf02576 (4967B/136c/26e), mem_e74ff47f (3404B/70c/0e), mem_3d2e572d (3028B/53c/0e), mem_a36a4735 (1923B/40c/4e), mem_11a5a1b3 (1914B/38c/6e). Access note: raw bytes via eng.substrate.get(record.content_hash); eng.get_raw(memory_id) raises KeyError (expects content hash, not memory id).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: It all started August last year when we met at a festival and he said he wanted to collaborate. (mem_0274eeefff094357, c9_session_24)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0/2187 claims have valid_at differing >1 day from the source record's session date, and 0 claims contain 'August' at all. Every 'yesterday'/'last week'/'last Friday'/'next month' phrase (69 claims carry them as raw text) inherits the session timestamp, so any 'when did X happen' question and any as_of/value_as_of time-travel query answers with the conversation date, not the event date. Record-level date detection works (22/30 records have non-empty metadata.dates) but never propagates to claims.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I restored a car last year, but I sold it to a collector. Now I'm working on something new (mem_90cc7a7fb11d407d, c9_session_25)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Zero claims or edges contain 'sold' (only a junk echo fragment 'Dave' --'i restored a car'--> 'a collector'). The graph has 6 'owns' edges with no supersession, so 'does Dave still have the car he restored?' will verify TRUE against a stale ownership fact -- exactly the verified-wrong failure mode already flagged for numeric aggregation, here for entity state.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relationship edges for most records (graph coverage gap)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I booked a flight ticket to Boston last week! I'm so excited about my upcoming trip to Boston. (mem_bb17284982cb40a4, c9_session_17 -- record has 0 edges)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 17/30 records contribute zero edges (79 edges total for 2187 claims). Facts from those sessions exist only as flat claims, so multi-hop/bridge-join recall and fact_history over relations (person->place, person->person) are blind for more than half the sessions.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Ever since I was ten, I've been fascinated with how machines work. (mem_90cc7a7fb11d407d, c9_session_25)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The age survives only as a junk fragment claim 'Dave' --'was ten'--> "ten, I've been fascinated..."; the claim 'unit' field is empty across the store and no measure-typed claim exists, so 'how old was Dave when he started working on cars' depends on lexical luck rather than a typed value, and numeric verification has nothing to check against.

raw_extraction.notes: Not dead: all 30 records extracted (39-128 claims each, median ~66), and the salient narrative facts (events, preferences, plans, negations like 'has not tried ramen yet', itemized lists like 'sunsets, beaches, waves') mostly have decent LLM-extracted claims. Verdict is patchy because whole classes are systematically absent: (1) event-time backdating is 0% -- claims universally inherit session valid_at; (2) disposal/state-change verbs (sold) never become claims/edges; (3) 57% of records yield zero edges. Noise caveat: ~24% of claims (514/2187) are per-sentence echo fallbacks with non-entity subjects ('Been', \"Can't\", 'Take', full dialogue lines as objects) at confidence=1.0 -- confidence is uninformative (all claims conf=1.0), which inflates the 2187 claim count and pollutes subject-indexed lookup. Audited store: DATA_DIR=/Users/raunakgengiti/Eidetic-Plus/artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data, namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g7-r0; sampled mem_bb17284982cb40a4, mem_b5fe70926c544473, mem_85942cb65d3b4394, mem_d84edda587ba490d, mem_0274eeefff094357, mem_90cc7a7fb11d407d, mem_600efdfd3f3946b2, mem_135d370e6e58485c.
raw_extraction.zero_extraction_cause: n/a -- extraction is firing on all 30 records; no zero-extraction records observed

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: cross-turn Q/A-resolved facts (locations + dated events confirmed by an answer turn)

missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: In mem_5360a0c317e045f5 the Banff question sentence has NO claim at all (verified uncovered) and Evan's 'Yeah' answer is stored without the location, so the fact 'Evan skied in Banff last month' does not exist as any claim or edge from this record. LoCoMo asks exactly this shape ('where did X go last month'); verify-or-abstain must abstain or cite the wrong record. The only 2 Banff claims in the namespace come from a different record (mem_68ac332624a04bdf).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: By the way, I've started a new diet—limiting myself to just two ginger snaps a day.

Numbers survive only embedded in raw-sentence state/event claims ('two ginger snaps a day', '1968 Kustom K-200A'), so the deterministic numeric path (structured_recall / measure_sum) cannot find or aggregate them — this feeds directly into the known 5/13 verified-WRONG numeric-aggregation failure mode.

missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 0/1753 claims have a non-empty relation — every claim is subject + raw sentence — and only 6/25 records produced any non-inferred edges (90 total, 20 of which are sourceless co_activated). The gift relation above exists only as prose; entity-relation time-travel (value_as_of/fact_history) and graph-hop recall are keyed on relations, so they are blind to 19 of 25 records.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Nature's beauty captivates me and brings me peace.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 43 claims store the literal string 'True' instead of content, concentrated in 4 records (28 of mem_5360a0c317e045f5's 108 claims — 26% of its payload; 10 in mem_641f550ef7b2463d). That record also has 13 substantive sentences with zero textual claim coverage (the quote is one). NLI cannot entail an answer against 'True', so these facts are silently unrecallable and unverifiable.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g8-r0 (r15 window): 25 records, 1753 claims, 90 edges confirmed. Every record has claims (range 40-108, roughly proportional to length), and most substantive sentences ARE present as raw-sentence claims (1968 guitar, ginger-snaps diet, Icefields Parkway, 'used to love hiking' all findable), so this is not dead. Temporal anchoring works: all 155/155 event claims carry valid_at, and relative dates get resolved (e.g. 'yesterday' -> a 2023-12-04 event claim in mem_dfb53ca99e9d4eaf). Verdict is PATCHY because extraction is essentially speaker-attributed sentence splitting, not structuring: claim-type mix is 1465 state / 155 event / 111 preference / 19 interval / 3 quantity; relation is empty on all 1753 claims; 19/25 records have zero edges; 43 claims are 'True' placeholders; many subjects are non-entities ('It's', 'Take', 'But', 'Here's', 'Letting', 'By'), which breaks entity-keyed retrieval. Retractions exist only as untyped prose ('My old prius broke down, decided to get it repaired and sell it' is a plain state claim, no supersession), but fewer negation-bearing sentences exist in this namespace (2 found), so ranked below the four classes above. Reproduce with: DATA_DIR=artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data .venv/bin/python + Engine/Scope snippet from the task; key records: /Users/raunakgengiti/Eidetic-Plus/artifacts/holdout_rotation_r15_codex/data (mem_5360a0c317e045f5, mem_641f550ef7b2463d, mem_dfb53ca99e9d4eaf, mem_68ac332624a04bdf).

Same record's 'working with fashion bloggers and influencers in the next few months' is also fragment-only. LoCoMo 'when did/will X' questions and as_of temporal recall cannot resolve these; the deterministic structured_recall path sees no dated event at all. Contrast: the studio opening 'tomorrow' in mem_e46db307 WAS extracted, so the extractor can do this class but misses it inconsistently.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Jon: Hey Gina, I had to shut down my bank account. It was tough, but I needed to do it for my biz. (mem_f72a7d24)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Zero well-formed claim for the shutdown — only fragment '[state] Jon | jon hey gina i | shut down my bank account' plus a vacuous '[event] Jon | needed | to do it'. Verify-or-abstain relies on typed retractions/supersessions to answer 'does Jon still have his bank account' with No; without one, latest-assertion-wins has nothing to supersede and the store silently keeps any earlier has-account implication.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Besides the dance classes and workshops, I'm offering one-on-one mentoring and training to help dancers reach their full potential. (mem_41b9438b)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Only one claim, covering just 'one-on-one mentoring and training' — dance classes and workshops are never itemized. Same pattern in mem_f72a7d24 (Jon's 3-part expansion: social media, school workshops, dance competition — fragments only) and mem_3b2ef86f (Gina's marketing channels Instagram/TikTok/influencer collabs — fragments only). List-recall questions ('what does Jon's studio offer?') undercount, and the claim_list_enum carve-out from the cost blitz has nothing to enumerate.

Edge extraction ran on only 9/19 records, and 16/92 edges carry an EMPTY source_memory_id (unattributable). value_as_of/fact_history entity-relation time travel and graph recall are blind on half the sessions, and the orphan edges cannot be cited or verified against a source record.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-locomo-g9-r0 (19 recs / 1258 claims / 92 active edges; the 93rd is expired or inferred). Sampled 8 records spanning shortest (1525 chars) to longest (4516 chars). Extraction is bimodal: each record gets ~5-12 well-formed semantic claims (headline biography lands: Door Dash job loss, Jon ex-banker, tattoo 'a few years ago', online store launch, studio opening 'tomorrow' with edge) PLUS a 60-70% tail of n-gram shingle fragments (848/1258 by heuristic: subjects 'Been'/'It's'/'Your'/'c1_session_N', predicates like 'jon hey gina hope' = lowercased 4-word text shingles). The 1258 figure therefore overstates real coverage ~3x, and the fragment claims pollute the typed-claim (structured_recall/SMQE) path with wrong-subject rows. Edge coverage is the sharpest gap: 10/19 records emitted zero edges (incl. 4068-char and 3780-char records dense with relations), 16 edges have empty source_memory_id (provenance/citation gap), and one observed mis-relation: 'Jon --is located in--> Rome' for a one-week trip (would corrupt location questions). Verdict patchy, not healthy: whole classes (dated future plans, cessation events, list itemization, edges on half the records) are systematically absent while headline facts are covered.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: last week I posted a photo from my hike and it got a ton of engagement - 237 likes and 34 comments
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: mem_e6b835e77631480b (204 claims) has only 4 'quantity' claims and every one carries the full sentence as value with unit='' and no parsed number ('237', '34', '10 new followers' live only inside untyped state-claim text, e.g. the Negroni '10 times now' count is an untyped state claim with subject 'Syrup'). Deterministic count/sum recall (measure_sum, structured_recall) cannot aggregate over prose values -- this is exactly the fact class behind the live LME-S 5/13 verified-WRONG numeric-aggregation headline (#41/#42).

Rachel appears only in untyped sentence claims with junk subjects ('Decorations', 'Your', 'Providing'), so a 'who is the user's sister' or 'who photographed the party' question has no typed anchor.

Relative-date facts ('last week I posted', 'past month') get no calendar entry, so temporal-index recall and as_of time-travel over events fall back to raw-text search only; the 95 'event' claims that exist are sentence blobs with wrong subjects (e.g. subj='Instagram' for a user action).

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: For the payment term, I would like request for 30% - 40% (halfway) - 30% (completion), is this workable ?
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: mem_39cb8a337ea64d1a: the three-stage payment schedule is captured only as one untyped preference claim holding raw text -- no list/enum structure (the claim_list_enum carve-out fired on just 2 of 5245 claims namespace-wide). Ordinal/list-enumeration questions ('what was the second payment stage', 'list the payment terms') have no deterministic path and must re-read raw prose.

raw_extraction.zero_extraction_cause: Only EDGES are zero (claims=5245, not zero). Cause: intentional long-haystack cost breaker, not a crash. The bench env (run_manifest.json) set CONSOLIDATION_EXTRACT_CALL_BUDGET=90 + CONSOLIDATION_LONG_HAYSTACK_RAW_ONLY=1; the 48-record batch's planned extraction windows exceeded the 0.8*budget trigger (72), so consolidate_pending() (eidetic/engine.py ~1450-1480) entered raw_only_long_haystack mode with remaining_budget=0 -> window allowance 0 -> client.extract_edges() never called -> triples=[] -> graph.add_fact never runs -> 0 edges AND 0 EventRecords. 47/48 records carry metadata consolidation_raw_only='batch_window_budget'. The 5245 claims all come from the token-free deterministic fallback (eidetic/smqe/claim_extraction.py claims_for_record), which sentence-shreds: 3866/5245 (74%) are tagged untyped=1 with junk subjects ('Here', 'And', 'The', 'Correct', 'Syrup').
raw_extraction.notes: Store: DATA_DIR=artifacts/lme_s_r1_codex/data, namespace eidetic-plus-full-longmemeval-g0-r0. 48 records / 5245 claims / 0 edges / 0 events / 0 inferred edges. Sampled 8+ records (4 shortest 425-1756 chars, 4 longest 18k-22k chars, plus 3 mid-length relationship/negation hits). Coverage is broad but shallow: every record has claims (min 5, max 207; 0 records with zero claims), so recall-by-text works, but claim TYPE distribution is state=4505/pref=447/interval=112/event=95/quantity=86 and no claim carries a parsed numeric value or unit anywhere sampled. Negations ARE captured as text ('Emma Taylor and \"The Lost City\" are not real' -> state claim pred='are not'), so retraction class is present-but-unlinked rather than missing. extraction_health=patchy: whole classes (edges/relations, events, parsed quantities, list structure) are absent while raw sentence claims cover everything. Fix lever: re-run consolidation with CONSOLIDATION_EXTRACT_CALL_BUDGET=0 (bench/run.py:310 already suggests this) or raise the budget above planned windows; the deterministic claim extractor separately needs numeric value/unit parsing to serve aggregation queries. Key files: eidetic/engine.py (ingest ~line 214 extract_edges, consolidate_pending ~1440-1700), eidetic/smqe/claim_extraction.py (~line 207 untyped tag), eidetic/config.py:497-509 (budget knobs), artifacts/lme_s_r1_codex/run_manifest.json (env proof).

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relative dated events (not resolved to event time)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I've had some experience with lavender in baking, like when I made a lemon Lavender cake two weeks ago for my friend's birthday party
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The event claim (user | made | lemon lavender cake, rec mem_41b2d865f5084493) carries valid_at 2023-05-20T07:54 == the session timestamp, not ~2023-05-06. Every relative expression ('two weeks ago', 'last week', 'yesterday') is anchored to session time, so the bi-temporal path (value_as_of, as_of recall, temporal-reasoning LME questions) answers the wrong date with full confidence -- and NLI can't catch it because the proof_atom entails the sentence.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: I've written five short stories so far, and I'm hoping to keep the momentum going.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Record mem_6b76e523edd34e1b has 142 claims but 0 quantity-type claims; 'five' only survives as state claims under garbage subject 'Example'. Digit forms ('17 poems') do get typed quantity claims, so the deterministic numeric path (measure_sum -- the exact surface behind open #41/#42 over-sum bugs) silently sees a partial universe: counts written as words are invisible to aggregation while digit counts are summed, guaranteeing scope/count errors that verify=True can't flag.

missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: user: I appreciate your suggestions, but I still don't believe that the Lewisham Council is doing enough to make a difference.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: 249 claims have negated proof_atoms; many triples strip the negation -- this one (rec mem_522409efd0544fd4) is stored as 'user | is doing | doing enough to make a difference', the affirmative opposite. Any consumer of subject/predicate/object instead of proof_atom (structured_recall, value_as_of, edge inference, retraction detection) asserts the inverse of what the user said; only the NLI-on-proof_atom path is safe.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: relationships between people (person edges absent, subject misattribution)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: She told me about her family's journey from Casablanca to Paris, and eventually, to the United States.
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The 103 edges cover assistant encyclopedia content (Kwisatz Haderach--possesses-->prescience, France--colonized-->Morocco) but zero edges for Amira, the user's friend, or the user's own social graph (rec mem_63b80c5d743e4e0a: Amira appears only inside state-claim text under subjects 'France'/'It\'s'/'You'). Only 108/5118 claims have subject 'user'; the rest sit under chunk-id junk ('sharegpt_yoSUjfa_5', 'Example', 'Mechanics'), so entity-keyed recall about people ('who moved from Casablanca?', 'whose birthday was the cake for?') has nothing to key on.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-longmemeval-g1-r0 (DATA_DIR=artifacts/lme_s_r1_codex/data): 53 records, 5118 claims, 74 strict/103 incl-inferred edges; all 53 records have >=1 claim (min 2, max 258), zero dead records. Sampled 11 records across the length distribution (59B-23KB). RAW/LEXICAL coverage is dense to the point of near-verbatim segmentation: content-word probes over every user-turn sentence in 8 sampled records show ~95% COV at a 0.7 threshold, and proof_atoms preserve full sentences -- the free-read + NLI verify path has almost nothing missing. The 'patchy' verdict is about the TYPED layer the deterministic paths consume: claim_type skews 4446 state / 307 preference / 182 interval / 96 event / 79 quantity / 8 table, and the four classes above (relative-date resolution, word-form quantities, negation polarity in triples, person edges/subjects) are systematically absent or corrupted there. Extra signal: many quantity claims are themselves degenerate (value = whole sentence, unit empty, subject 'Wrap'/'Bake'/'Allow'), and top claim subjects are chunk ids not entities -- consistent with the 2026-07-09 headline that the deterministic numeric path ships verified-wrong sums. Caution for re-runs: a shell typo that drops DATA_DIR silently falls back to the default store, which ALSO contains this namespace with different records -- easy contamination vector for audits.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: Decimal quantities truncated at the period (corrupted values stored as claims)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: Tide Original Scent Laundry Detergent (100 oz) is on sale for $11.99 (reg. $14.99)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: The stored claim object is literally 'on sale for $11' -- the sentence splitter breaks on the period inside the number and the cents are silently dropped. 14 claims across 6+ records end mid-decimal (mem_97bd40e0 '$11'/'$14'/'$2' for $11.99/$14.99/$2.99; mem_8c1542fd '$11,721' for $11,721.xx court award; mem_8ea45fbb 'approximately 3' for 3.4 meters; mem_05537ba6 'f/2' for f/2.8; mem_8ab22d0a 'PM2' for PM2.5). The deterministic claim path (measure_sum/structured_recall) reads these objects as truth, so any numeric aggregation over them is wrong-by-construction, and NLI verification passes it (entailment cannot catch missing cents) -- this is the same verified-WRONG family as open issues #41/#42.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: Quantity-to-entity binding lost in itemized price/deal lists
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: * Up & Up Paper Towels (12-roll pack) is on sale for $14.99 (reg. $19.99)
missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: Every price claim in mem_97bd40e0 has subject = the raw session id 'a7c62fd2_2' ('a7c62fd2_2 | is on | on sale for $14'), not the product. Nine bullet-list prices are detached from what they price, so 'how much were the paper towels on sale for' has zero claim support even though a claim technically exists; retrieval either abstains or grabs the wrong product's number. Combined with class 1 the itemized-list numeric answers are doubly broken.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: JSON-embedded payload bodies (transcripts/structured data) -- near-zero tail extraction

missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: mem_ddaf9583 (8447 bytes) got only 17 claims, all from the prompt instructions plus the first ~5% of the transcript; 101 of 106 numbers in the record appear in no claim (270-mile range, 600 vs 500 hp, four-second 0-60, regen-to-6-mph), and the negation 'most other cars, when I open the door, they turn the music volume down. This one doesn't' is also unextracted. Whole records whose facts live inside JSON payloads are effectively invisible to claim-based recall and force abstention or free-read fallback.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: Ordinal position in enumerated lists (items kept, rank dropped)

missed_fact_classes.why_it_matters: mem_745f5d7b stores all 20 product-name list items as bare identity claims ('GastricGuru | gastricguru | GastricGuru') but no claim binds any item to its list index, and the same pattern repeats in mem_457d861b and mem_5f4a5d35 (indices 10-20 absent from every claim). Enumeration questions ('what was the 16th suggestion', 'how many names were proposed') cannot be answered or verified from claims -- this is the known ordinal-list backlog item showing up live.

raw_extraction.notes: Namespace eidetic-plus-full-longmemeval-g10-r0 (DATA_DIR=artifacts/lme_s_r1_codex/data): 53 records, 4933 claims, 85 non-inferred edges (manifest said 88; 85 returned by all_edges include_inferred=False). Extraction is NOT dead: every record has claims, density ~1 claim/90 bytes, and a full numeric+negation sweep shows most records with 0 missing numbers and negations mostly captured (e.g. mem_e6a123e1 12 negations in text / 9 in claims). Long evidence sessions capture user-personal facts well (mem_2ad348ad 17.6KB/188 claims: 'gift from my sister -- beautiful watercolor painting', 'framing it last weekend', 'bought on sale last year' all present). The reason for 'patchy' rather than 'healthy' is class-level, not volume-level: (1) a deterministic decimal-truncation bug corrupts every claim whose object ends at a number containing a period (14 confirmed via source-text cross-check, script pattern: claim object ends in digits while source continues '.digit'), (2) itemized price claims lose their product subject, (3) JSON-heavy records are ~95% unextracted past the first lines, (4) list rank is never encoded. Classes 1+2 are the most consequential because they feed the verify-or-abstain numeric path that already ships verified-WRONG answers (2026-07-09 headline, issues #41/#42): the corruption happens at WRITE time, so no amount of read-side gating can recover the cents or the product binding. Edges are sparse (85 edges from only 8 of 53 records) but claims carry the load. Repro scripts in scratchpad: audit.py (per-record text+claims dump), gap.py (missing-number/negation scan), trunc.py (decimal-truncation counter).

raw_extraction.zero_extraction_cause: PREMISE CORRECTION: claims are NOT zero -- 5,135 active claims exist, min 6 / median 110 / max 208 per record, 0 records claimless. What IS zero: graph edges AND events. Cause (proven from record metadata): the run had CONSOLIDATION_EXTRACT_CALL_BUDGET set; 45/46 g11 records carry metadata consolidation_raw_only='batch_window_budget', meaning the consolidate_pending() batch exhausted its LLM extraction-window budget before g11's records were reached, so client.extract_edges (the only producer of Edge + EventRecord rows, engine.py ~1600) never ran for them. The single record that DID get a window (len 14450, no raw_only stamp) returned zero triples (entities=[]). Claims survive because SMQE claims_for_record (eidetic/smqe/claim_extraction.py, regex/sentence-based, no LLM) runs per record in Pass 1 regardless of the window allowance. Same-store confirmation: 7 of 30 namespaces (g0,g8,g11,g13,g17,g21,g25) have 0 fact edges; namespaces like g2/g5/g9/g12/g26 show exactly 10 edges that are ALL relation='co_activated' (recall-time link_memories, not extraction). Extraction was never 'off' -- it was budget-starved. Fix: rerun consolidation for these namespaces with CONSOLIDATION_EXTRACT_CALL_BUDGET=0 (unlimited) or a budget >= planned windows.

missed_fact_classes.fact_class: entity-keyed subjects (claims keyed to junk anchors)
missed_fact_classes.example_record_quote: claim: subj='Oats' pred='i take the bus' value='I take the bus to work and it takes around 45 minutes.' -- and subjects like 'However', 'Here', "Here's", '6c16c3ec_3' throughout
